pages.title: Peter de Lory — Photographer
pages.description: Black-and-white photographs by Peter de Lory: the American West, the Pacific Northwest, gelatin silver landscapes. Held by the Smithsonian, Walker Art Center, and SFMOMA.

pages.title: About — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Peter de Lory, born 1948 — a romantic structuralist. BFA San Francisco Art Institute, MFA University of Colorado; NEA Fellowship; fifty years of photographing the American West.

pages.title: Survey — Peter de Lory
pages.description: A chronological index of Peter de Lory's fifty-year career: series, exhibitions, publications, commissions, awards, and residencies.

pages.title: Catalogue — Peter de Lory
pages.description: The full record of Peter de Lory's exhibitions, public collections, publications, and awards — filterable and sortable.

pages.title: Commissions — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Public art and commissioned work by Peter de Lory: Champion at Sea-Tac Airport, Sound Transit's Railwork, Seattle Public Utilities' Citywork, and more.

pages.title: Contact — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Inquire to acquire work by Peter de Lory. Represented by Harris Harvey Gallery, Seattle.

pages.title: Notable Works & Recognition — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Fifty years, fifteen public collections, an NEA Fellowship, and an Aperture frontispiece under Minor White. The record of Peter de Lory's work.

pages.title: Back Pages — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Back Pages (1964–1965): Peter de Lory's earliest, most personal work, made on Cape Cod.

pages.title: Pacific Heights Mansions — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Pacific Heights Mansions: soft-focus, surrealist photographs of San Francisco that Tony Ray-Jones called genuinely original — later the frontispiece of Aperture 17:2.

pages.title: Landscape One — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Landscape One (1978): early landscape work using the land as a metaphor for emotion.

pages.title: Western Neon Signs — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Western Neon Signs: a twenty-year documentary project photographing the disappearing neon of the American West, in color and black-and-white.

pages.title: Still Lifes — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Still Lifes: ongoing large-format 4×5 still-life work spanning Peter de Lory's career.

pages.title: The Edge of Town — Peter de Lory
pages.description: The Edge of Town (1987–1993): the arid American West along Nevada's Route 50, after Wallace Stegner's Beyond the Hundredth Meridian.

pages.title: Once Upon a Time in the West — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Once Upon a Time in the West (1988–2004): triptychs on Western mythology and ideology, after Edward Abbey, Wallace Stegner, and Cormac McCarthy.

pages.title: Peopled Landscape — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Peopled Landscape by Peter de Lory.

pages.title: Big Rock Candy Mountain — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Big Rock Candy Mountain by Peter de Lory.

pages.title: Henry Real Bird — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Henry Real Bird (~1995): the O-W Ranch and the Crow Nation, Montana.

pages.title: Tenuous Remnants — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Tenuous Remnants (2012): tipi rings of the Pryor Mountains, sacred prayer sites that address the stars, and the marks of westward expansion.

pages.title: Half Memory — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Half Memory: the unrolling landscape of the American West, framed through car windows.

pages.title: Somewhere Along the Way — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Somewhere Along the Way: the automobile as framing device, from the Three Exhibitions at Lisa Harris Gallery.

pages.title: Balance — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Balance: three exhibitions at Lisa Harris Gallery, Seattle.

pages.title: A Silent Way — Peter de Lory
pages.description: A Silent Way (2017–2018): gelatin silver photographs of Pacific Northwest water and the Rosario Strait crossing.

pages.title: The Falls — Peter de Lory
pages.description: The Falls (2009–2011): long-exposure waterfalls that become elemental forces. Exhibited at Lisa Harris Gallery, January 2011.

pages.title: The Cape — Peter de Lory
pages.description: The Cape (1964–early 1980s): Cape Cod aerials and landscapes, after Edward Hopper — where Peter de Lory's work began.

pages.title: The Rock — Peter de Lory
pages.description: The Rock by Peter de Lory.

pages.title: Champion — Sea-Tac Airport — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Champion (2003–2013): Peter de Lory photographed the world's tallest Western Red Cedar; the image became a 17-foot mosaic permanently sited at Sea-Tac Airport.

pages.title: Railwork — Sound Transit — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Railwork (2000–2013): thirteen years documenting Sound Transit building five light rail stations from Seattle to Tacoma.

pages.title: Citywork — Seattle Public Utilities — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Citywork (1999–2000): 170 photographs of the entirety of Seattle Public Utilities, permanently installed throughout SPU facilities.

pages.title: Seattle Water Department — Peter de Lory
pages.description: Seattle Water Department (1996): nine months tracing the path of water from the Cascade Mountains to the city's faucets.

pages.title: A Pathway — Peter de Lory
pages.description: A Pathway (2015): nineteen images made in and around Hayes Freedom High School, Camas, Washington, for the State Art Collection.
